JASH ENGINEERING – MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY

• Targeting PAT margins of ~13–14%

• Aiming for EBITDA margins in the range of ~21–24%

• Increase in depreciation has contributed to higher US revenue visibility

• Business largely insulated from West Asia geopolitical risks due to limited exposure

Info Edge turned ₹3,950 crore into ₹36,850 crore through investments in 111 startups since 2007 - a mind boggling 36% IRR

But, 90% of this value (₹31,500 crore) comes from 2 companies - PB FinTech (₹8,500 crore) & Eternal (₹23,000 crore) which were investments made in 2008 & 2010.

When you remove these outliers, the remaining ₹2,875 crore invested by IE is marked at approx. ₹5,350 crore (a little less than x2)

Mr Bikhchandani wrote an excellent shareholder letter which shared 3 lessons about startup investing:

(1) The Power Law of Returns

(2) Vintage Matters - Children become Adults over time

(3) Staying Power is a Superpower

Lets dive into these lessons

FERTILISER SECTOR – POLICY SHIFT & SUPPLY SHOCK ⚠️

• Govt pushing alternatives to chemical fertilisers after ~24.6% output drop in March
• Disruption linked to West Asia tensions impacting supply chains

Policy Action Plan
• Led by Indian Council of Agricultural Research
• Targeting top 100 high-consumption districts (urea, DAP)
• Focus on measurable reduction in chemical fertiliser usage

Reduction Targets
• ~5% reduction in FY27 season
• ~25% reduction by 2030

Why This Crisis Emerged
• Iran conflict disrupted supplies via Strait of Hormuz
• Impacted imports of fertilisers + raw materials

• LNG shortages hit domestic production
→ Qatar (key LNG supplier) declared force majeure
→ Several plants shut → output collapse

Short-Term Govt Response
• Spot LNG purchases at higher cost
• Increased allocation to fertiliser companies

• Subsidy burden expected to rise by ₹20,000–25,000 Cr (estimates)

Kharif Risk
• Disruption ahead of key sowing season (June start)
• Affects crops: rice, pulses, oilseeds, cotton, maize, sugarcane
• Risk of food inflation if supply tightens

Alternatives Being Promoted
• Organic inputs: compost, farmyard manure
• Biofertilisers: microbe-based nutrients
• Water-soluble inputs: nano-urea, micronutrients

Key Insight
• India’s fertiliser chain exposed at 3 levels: imports, LNG dependency, and timing risk
• Policy pivot underway: reduce structural dependence on imports

Bottom Line
• Short term: higher subsidies + cost pressures
• Medium term: shift towards sustainable inputs and supply diversification

text:
5G TURNS 4 IN INDIA — SCALE HIGH, EXPERIENCE MIXED 📶

Rollout Scale — Massive
• India has deployed 5,00,000+ 5G base stations
• Coverage expanded to almost all districts
• One of the fastest 5G rollouts globally

Operator Positioning

• Reliance Jio
→ ~250M+ 5G users (>50% subscriber base)
→ Nationwide Standalone (SA) network — more advanced architecture
→ Clear leader in coverage + scale

• Bharti Airtel
→ Strong urban rollout comparable to Jio
→ Uses Non-Standalone (NSA) network (built on 4G core)
→ Faster/cheaper rollout but limited advanced 5G capabilities

• Vodafone Idea
→ Late entrant; limited to few dozen cities
→ Plans expansion to ~100+ cities
→ Struggling to compete with larger peers

The Real Experience Gap

• Urban areas: strong and stable 5G performance
• Highways/outskirts: patchy coverage, signal drops

• Real-world usage depends on tower proximity, not just “coverage claims”

Regulatory Gap

• Telecom Regulatory Authority of India mandates coverage maps
• But maps use permissive thresholds → don’t reflect actual usability

• Drive tests show wide variation in:
→ Speeds
→ Signal quality

• Current metrics (call drops, uptime) = compliance-based, not experience-based

Key Insight

• India’s 5G rollout = infrastructure success
• But user experience = inconsistent outside dense urban zones

What Next?

• Focus shifts from expansion → quality & consistency
• Unlock SA-led use cases:
→ Enterprise networks
→ Fixed wireless broadband
→ IoT & private networks

Bottom Line

• Built fast, scaled big — now needs to deliver consistent real-world performance
• Next phase of 5G will be defined by experience, not just coverage

GANESHA ECOSPHERE – rPET POLICY PLAY

• Ganesha Ecosphere Limited key beneficiary of rPET/EPR policy
• rPET = recycled PET used in bottles, packaging, polyester

• 2022 PWM Rules mandate recycled plastic usage (EPR)
• FMCG giants required to use minimum recycled content
• Creates structural, long-term demand for rPET

• FY23: Shift from rPSF to food-grade rPET (Go Rewise)
• FY24: Warangal rPET line commissioned, QIP raised
• FY25: 30% rPET mandate; capacity 14K → 42K TPA
• FY26: 40% mandate effective Apr 2026
• FY27–29: Target rises up to 60% (Category I)

• Phase 1 (FY22–24): Stock re-rating post policy
• Phase 2 (FY25–26): Correction due to costs, ramp-up
• Phase 3 (FY27+): Demand-policy alignment with capacity

• Market size ₹10.67 Bn (2023) → ₹17.5 Bn (2030)

• Key risks: scrap price volatility, execution delays
• rPSF segment pressure from cheap imports
• Policy dilution / implementation risks

Key Takeaway
• Policy-led demand + capacity readiness = earnings inflection

Impact: Positive – strong structural tailwind from regulation

Avendus on Indian Alcoholic Beverages — Sector Initiation
*Premiumization Drives Value-Led Growth | Broad-Based Buy Across Coverage*

**Sector Thesis**
- Alcohol: One of India's most resilient consumption categories
- Demand anchored in habit, social occasions, and rising aspiration
- Strong entry barriers via state regulation, high taxation, controlled distribution
- Premiumization shifting industry from volume story to value-led growth
- Better brands and experiences driving consumer upgrades across segments
- Stronger margin potential as premium mix rises across portfolios

**Key Sector Characteristics**
- Heavy state-level regulation creates natural competitive moats
- Controlled route-to-market structures limit new entrant disruption
- High taxation embeds pricing power for established brand owners
- Category resilience tested across cycles — demand remains sticky

**Ratings & Target Prices**
- United Spirits (USL): Buy | TP ₹1,662
- Radico Khaitan: Buy | TP ₹4,177
- Tilaknagar Industries: Buy | TP ₹550
- Allied Blenders & Distillers: Buy | TP ₹671
- United Breweries: Add | TP ₹1,837

**Avendus Investment Stance**
- Broad-based initiations signal high conviction on sector re-rating
- Premiumization theme central to all five coverage additions
- Four outright Buys; United Breweries initiated with Add
- Value-led growth with margin expansion seen as multi-year theme
- Regulatory moats and brand loyalty underpin long-term investor interest

JPMorgan on Yes Bank — Q4 FY26 Brokerage Note
Underweight Maintained | Valuation Limits Upside Despite Beat

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Underweight (UW) maintained
- Target Price: ₹18 per share

**Q4 FY26 Result Snapshot**
- PAT: ₹10.7 billion, +45% YoY, +12% QoQ
- PAT came 9% above JPMorgan estimates — a clear beat
- Opex rose just 2% YoY; came 2% below JPMe — tight cost control
- Credit costs at 28bps; 4bps lower than JPMe

**Key Positives**
- First time RoA hit 1% since bank reconstruction
- Cost-to-Income Ratio (CIR) improved to 63% during the quarter
- Credit costs remained contained at 28bps vs. JPMe of 32bps
- Operating leverage visible with opex growth well below revenue growth

**Key Concerns**
- RoA of 1% supported by 31bps contribution from SR (Stressed Asset) recoveries
- Sustainability of 1% RoA without SR tailwinds remains unproven
- CIR at 63% remains elevated vs. peers; continues to weigh on RoA
- MSME portfolio at risk from supply chain disruptions
- Credit costs likely to inch higher as MSME stress emerges

**Valuation View**
- Stock trading at 1.2x P/B FY27E
- JPM believes current valuation already prices in balance sheet growth
- RoA improvement potential also largely baked into price
- Limited re-rating catalyst visible at current levels

**JPM Investment Stance**
- Beat acknowledged but structural concerns persist
- SR recovery-driven RoA not a sustainable earnings quality signal
- Underweight stance retained; TP ₹18 implies limited upside
- Key monitorable: Visibility on RoA sustainability ex-SR recoveries

Overall, believe merger is EPS-accretive on an immediate basis

Cash is King, Profit is Opinion

A company can be profitable on paper but still go bust if its wallet is empty. The fundamental disconnect lies in accrual accounting, where revenue is recorded when a sale happens, even if the cash hasn't arrived yet. Data shows that in FY25, 729 profit-making companies actually had negative cash flow from operations, while 140 loss-making ones remained flush with cash.

To judge true financial health, investors must look at the Cash Flow Statement, specifically Operating Activities. This metric strips away non-cash accounting adjustments like depreciation to reveal the actual liquidity generated by the core business. While profit reflects potential, cash flow reflects the immediate reality of whether a firm can pay its bills, employees, and shareholders. Always follow the money, not just the math.

AI’s New Tycoons: The 21st Century Rockefeller?

A new era of "autocratic figures" has emerged, with Sam Altman, Demis Hassabis, Elon Musk, Dario Amodei, and Mark Zuckerberg holding the "fate of Western civilisation" in their hands. Much like the Gilded Age tycoons who monopolized railroads and oil, these five leaders control the distribution and evolution of a technology that is reshaping the world’s social and economic fabric.

However, a key difference remains: while 19th-century moguls like Rockefeller held sway over 1.5% of US GDP, modern AI leaders operate in a world where governments are already moving to curb their power. History suggests that while these "leading men" accelerate progress, their unchecked influence inevitably triggers a regulatory backlash—just as the Sherman Antitrust Act once dismantled Standard Oil. The question isn't if they are powerful, but whether they will face the same institutional "breakup" as their predecessors.
